issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not setting `aria-hidden="true"` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.close()` the drawer is hidden by CSS classes only; no `aria-hidden` is applied, so the drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: After hiding the drawer, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and optionally add `hidden` attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not setting `aria-hidden="false"` when opened
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.open()` the drawer becomes visible but `aria-hidden` is never removed or set to `"false"`, leaving screen readers to treat it as hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In the open routine, call `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` (or `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false')`) before adding the open class.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter status element lacks `aria-live`
issuesFound.explanation: The `<div class="newsletter-status">` used to announce success or error messages does not have an `aria-live` region, so updates are not announced to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: When creating the element, include `'aria-live': 'polite'` (or `'assertive'` for errors) and ensure it remains in the DOM.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard navigation buttons missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The “Previous”, “Next”, and “Submit” buttons control the wizard panels but have no `aria-controls` pointing to the associated step panels, breaking the ARIA tab‑panel pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Assign each button `aria-controls` with the `id` of the panel it shows (e.g., `setAttr(prevBtn, 'aria-controls', stepId)`).

issuesFound.title: Wizard step panels lack `aria-labelledby`
issuesFound.explanation: Panels are given `role="tabpanel"` and `aria-label`, but they are not linked to the corresponding tab button via `aria-labelledby`, which is required for the tab‑panel relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Give each tab button an `id` and set `setAttr(panelEl, 'aria-labelledby', tabId)`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle buttons missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that switch between grid and list view toggle the product grid but do not expose which element they control via `aria-controls`.
issuesFound.fix: After ensuring the grid has an `id`, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', gridId)` for each view‑mode button.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The “Previous” and “Next” buttons move the carousel but lack `aria-controls` linking them to the carousel track, preventing assistive tech from understanding the relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Assign each button `aria-controls` pointing to the carousel track element’s `id`.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload drop zone missing `aria-describedby` for status feedback
issuesFound.explanation: The drop zone announces status via a separate status element, but the zone does not reference that element with `aria-describedby`, so users of assistive technology may miss the feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After creating the status element, give it an `id` and add `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-describedby', statusId)`.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle button missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles light/dark/system theme sets `aria-pressed` but does not indicate which part of the page it controls (the `<html>` element’s `data-theme` attribute), violating the ARIA button pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Give the `<html>` element an `id` (e.g., `htmlRootId`) and add `setAttr(themeToggleBtn, 'aria-controls', htmlRootId)`.

issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu._openInstance() the panel is shown and CSS classes are added, but the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute is never set to true. Only the close_​instance() function resets it to false. This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: FAQ items lack an initial aria‑expanded attribute on their triggers
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are parsed ( faqRegistry.parse() ), the trigger button receives aria‑controls and aria‑labelledby, but no aria‑expanded attribute is set (neither true nor false). The attribute is only added later when a user interacts, leaving the initial state undefined for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
In faqRegistry.parse() after creating the trigger, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (or ensure the markup includes `aria-expanded="false"`).

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The helper srUtils.markInvalid() (which is used by inlineValidator to display error messages) adds an aria‑describedby reference but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the associated input/select/textarea. Consequently, screen‑reader users receive no programmatic indication that a field is invalid, even though visual error styling is applied.
issuesFound.fix: Modify srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl) to include `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`. Likewise, ensure srUtils.markValid() removes the attribute (`field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`). This will propagate to all inline‑validated fields across the site.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` (and its `open()` / `close()` helpers) add or remove CSS classes to show/hide the navigation drawer but never set or update the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close()` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button (`_trigger`) never receives an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the drawer element’s id. Without this relationship assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer has an id (e.g., `id="mobile-nav-drawer"`) and add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'mobile-nav-drawer')` during init.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open does not remove aria‑hidden from drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is opened (`open()`), the code adds CSS classes but never removes a possible `aria-hidden="true"` attribute from the drawer element (`_drawer`). The element remains hidden from the accessibility tree even though it is visible.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` call `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden')` after adding the open class.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not set aria‑hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed (`close()`), the code removes CSS classes but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer. The drawer stays in the accessibility tree while visually hidden, causing focusable content to be reachable.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()` call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` after removing the open class.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria‑controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button does not have an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the associated panel’s id. Without this, the relationship is ambiguous for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each trigger receives `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId)` where `panelId` is the panel’s id (generated if absent).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded on question button
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.open(item)`, the function expands the answer panel but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger element. Users of assistive technology cannot perceive the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria‑controls on init (if not present)
issuesFound.explanation: While parsing FAQ items, the code ensures `aria-controls` only if the trigger already has an id, but does not guarantee the attribute exists when the markup omits it. This can leave the relationship undefined.
issuesFound.fix: After generating ids for trigger and panel, always call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId)`.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input lacks aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The email field is bound with `inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required','email'])`. The inline validator adds error messages and CSS classes but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input when validation fails, violating WCAG 2.2 §4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Extend `inlineValidator` (or its render step) to call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` on error and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” field missing aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: Each contact form field is bound via `inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules)`. The validator does not set `aria-invalid` on the field when the rule fails, so screen‑reader users receive no programmatic indication of the error.
issuesFound.fix: Modify the inline validator’s error‑render path to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field and clear it when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons do not update `aria‑expanded` when opened or closed
issuesFound.explanation: The click/keyboard handlers toggle the panel’s visibility by adding/removing CSS classes and the `hidden` attribute, but they never set or clear `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the dropdown is open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons lack an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Because the buttons start closed, they should have `aria‑expanded="false"` in the markup or set during initialization; otherwise AT assumes the panel is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation trigger button lacks an initial `aria‑expanded` state
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button toggles the drawer via `setExpanded`, which updates `aria‑expanded` only after the first click. The button should start with `aria‑expanded="false"` so screen readers know the menu is collapsed initially.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation drawer is hidden with CSS but never marked `aria‑hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer’s visibility is controlled by CSS classes and `body` scroll‑lock, but AT can still navigate to its contents because `aria‑hidden` is never set.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are hidden with the `hidden` attribute but lack `aria‑hidden` when closed (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: Although the panel receives `hidden`, AT may still expose it if the element remains focusable. Adding `aria‑hidden="true"` guarantees it is removed from the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels are not announced to screen readers when opened or closed (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion updates visual state and ARIA attributes, but it never sends a live‑region announcement, so users of assistive technology are unaware of the change.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set `aria‑invalid` on invalid fields (JS‑D)
issuesFound.explanation: The validator only adds error‑message elements and CSS classes; it never toggles the required `aria‑invalid` attribute, leaving AT unaware of the error state.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not clear `aria‑invalid` when a field becomes valid (JS‑D, medium)
issuesFound.explanation: After an error is corrected, the field’s `aria‑invalid` attribute remains set, causing a persistent error indication for AT users.
issuesFound.fix:
(see Issue 7 – the `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')` line already clears it when `result.valid` is true).  

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: The badge text changes to reflect the number of matching products, but there is no `aria‑live` region to inform screen‑reader users of the new count.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation overlay is not hidden from AT when the drawer is closed (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay can still be focusable for assistive technologies, causing a focus trap.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update `aria‑expanded` on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer, but it never calls `setAttribute('aria‑expanded', …)` (or `.ariaExpanded`) on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is currently expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', String(!_open));` (or equivalent) inside both `open()` and `close()` so the attribute reflects the drawer state.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update `aria‑expanded` when opened/closed
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick` (and the related open/close helpers) the code toggles the panel’s visibility but never updates the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute. Assistive technologies will not receive the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true')`; after closing call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge` changes the text of the element with `[data-product-count]` but the element has no `aria‑live` attribute, so the change is invisible to AT. Users will not hear the new number of matching products.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria‑live', 'polite')` (or `role="status"` with `aria‑atomic="true"`) when the badge is rendered.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never sets `aria‑invalid` on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` marks fields as valid/invalid by adding CSS classes, but it never calls `setAttr(field, 'aria‑invalid', 'true')` (or removes it). Consequently, screen‑reader users receive no indication that a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix:
In the validation result handling, call `setAttr(field, 'aria‑invalid', 'true')` when `!result.valid` and `field.removeAttribute('aria‑invalid')` when valid.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger lacks initial `aria‑expanded="false"` on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) receives `aria‑controls` during init, but no `aria‑expanded` attribute is set to reflect the initial closed state. AT users therefore cannot know the menu is collapsed before any interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.init()` after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu triggers lack initial `aria‑expanded="false"` on page load
issuesFound.explanation: During `dropdownMenu.register()` the code sets `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls`, but does not initialise `aria‑expanded` to `"false"` for closed menus.
issuesFound.fix:
After `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);` also call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ question buttons lack initial `aria‑expanded="false"` on page load
issuesFound.explanation: `faqRegistry.parse()` sets `aria-controls` and `aria-labelledby` but does not set `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving its state undefined when the page first renders.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating the trigger, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` (or `"true"` if the item is pre‑opened).

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge does not have an accessible label describing its purpose
issuesFound.explanation: The badge only contains a number (e.g., “12”) without any `aria‑label` or `aria‑describedby`, so screen‑reader users hear only the number without context.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', `${count} matching product${count===1?'':'s'}`)` whenever the count changes.

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list does not expose a live region for result announcements
issuesFound.explanation: `suggestionListRenderer.mount()` creates a `<ul role="listbox">` but does not set `aria‑live` (or `role="status"`). When the list updates, AT users are not notified of the new suggestions.
issuesFound.fix: After creating the list element, add `setAttr(list, 'aria-live', 'polite')` (and optionally `aria‑atomic="true"`).

fpTitles: Product‑filter count badge does not have an accessible label describing its purpose

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions add/remove CSS classes and lock scrolling, but never set `aria‑expanded="true"` or `"false"` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the navigation drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true)` in `open()` and `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false)` in `close()`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks `aria‑controls` linking to the drawer element
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button does not reference the drawer via `aria‑controls`, so assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix:
After locating the drawer element, ensure it has an `id` (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')`) and set `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id)`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers lack `aria‑controls` linking to their panels
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button receives `aria‑haspopup="true"` but never receives `aria‑controls` pointing to the panel’s `id`. This breaks the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix:
When registering a dropdown, ensure the panel has an `id` (`srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel')`) and set `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, panel.id)`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not update `aria‑expanded` on their question buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Opening/closing an FAQ item adds/removes CSS classes and animates height, but never toggles `aria‑expanded` on the button (`item.trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether an answer is visible.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open(item)`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true)`; in `close(item)`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false)`.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle switch does not expose its state via `aria‑checked`
issuesFound.explanation: The element with `data‑billing‑toggle` acts as a binary switch (monthly ↔ annual) but is only given `role="switch"` without an accompanying `aria‑checked` state that reflects the current period. Assistive technologies cannot convey the current selection.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the period, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'))` (and set the opposite for `'month'`).

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter category tabs do not expose their selected state via `aria‑pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a filter tab updates CSS classes but never updates `aria‑pressed` on the button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the active category.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_onTabClick`, after determining the new active tab, set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', true)` and clear `aria-pressed` on the previously active button.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter category tabs lack `aria‑controls` linking to the product grid
issuesFound.explanation: The tabs control the visibility of the product grid but do not have `aria‑controls` pointing to the grid’s `id`. This breaks the explicit relationship required for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the grid has an `id` (`srUtils.ensureId(_gridEl, 'product-grid')`) and set `setAttr(tabButton, ARIA.CONTROLS, _gridEl.id)` for each tab.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing “Show annual” toggle button does not set `aria‑pressed` when toggled
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle that switches between monthly and annual billing is a button that changes state, yet the code never updates `aria‑pressed` to reflect the current selection.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'))`.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing “Show annual” toggle button lacks `aria‑controls` to the pricing cards it affects
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle changes the displayed prices on all plan cards, but the button does not reference those cards via `aria‑controls`. Users cannot discover which region is being manipulated.
issuesFound.fix:
Assign an `id` to the container that holds all plan cards (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_gridEl, 'pricing-cards')`) and set `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CONTROLS, _gridEl.id)`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation focus trap does not return focus to the trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed, the focus trap is deactivated but the previously focused element (the trigger) is not restored, leaving keyboard users stranded.
issuesFound.fix:
After deactivating the trap, call `_trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true })` or store the previously focused element and restore it.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not return focus to its trigger when closed via Esc or outside click
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown via Esc or clicking outside hides the panel but never moves focus back to the trigger button, breaking the expected keyboard navigation flow.
issuesFound.fix:
After hiding the panel, call `inst.trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true })`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “Open all” button does not set `aria‑expanded` on the newly opened items
issuesFound.explanation: The “Open all” control expands all FAQ items but does not update `aria‑expanded` on each item’s trigger, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new state.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside the loop that opens each item, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true)`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “Close all” button does not set `aria‑expanded` to false on each trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Closing all items removes CSS classes but does not reset `aria‑expanded` on the triggers, causing stale state information for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside the loop that closes each item, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false)`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay does not have `role="dialog"` or `aria‑modal` to indicate it blocks interaction with the rest of the page when open
issuesFound.explanation: When the overlay is shown, it visually blocks the page but lacks ARIA semantics that convey a modal context to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: When showing the overlay, set `setAttr(_overlay, 'role', 'dialog')` and `setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-modal', 'true')`; remove these attributes when hiding.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.toggle(item)` function expands or collapses an FAQ item but never updates the `aria-expanded` attribute on the question button (`item.trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know which answer is currently visible.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav button missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button that opens/closes the mobile navigation acts as a two‑state toggle (open / closed) but never receives an `aria-pressed` attribute, so AT cannot convey its state.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters()` updates the product count badge (`_countBadge.textContent`) but does not write to an `aria-live` region, so users of assistive technology are not informed when the number of visible products changes.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field does not set aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: In `newsletterForm._onSubmit()` the email address is validated with a regex, but when the check fails the code only shows a status message; it never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the `<input>` element, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the error.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded attribute (page‑load)
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads the navigation button (`_trigger`) does not have `aria-expanded="false"` set, so the initial collapsed state is not conveyed to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer is hidden with CSS only, no aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close()` removes the “open” class from the drawer but never adds `aria-hidden="true"` to the drawer element. Because the drawer remains in the accessibility tree while visually hidden, screen‑reader users can still navigate into it.

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle button does not announce the change via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter._onViewToggle()` switches the product grid between grid and list layouts, but no live‑region update informs users that the view mode has changed.

issuesFound.title: Pagination status is not announced to AT when the page changes
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter._syncPagination()` writes “Page X of Y” into the pagination element, but the element lacks an `aria-live` attribute, so screen‑reader users are not notified when pagination updates.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button used to open/close the mobile navigation drawer never receives an aria‑controls attribute that points to the drawer element’s id. Screen‑reader users cannot discover the relationship.
issuesFound.fix: After _navDrawer is identified, ensure setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id) (or generate an id for the drawer if missing).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the mobile‑nav trigger button lacks an aria‑expanded attribute, violating the rule that toggle widgets must have their ARIA state initialized.
issuesFound.fix: In the module’s init() or immediately after selecting _trigger, set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module’s _onTriggerClick handler toggles the dropdown panel’s visibility but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the panel’s state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after closing, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed, the code only adds a CSS class and sets hidden attribute, but does not set aria‑hidden="true". Without aria‑hidden, the panel remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In _closeInstance add setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true') when hiding, and remove it (or set 'false') when showing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion module’s open() and close() functions toggle the answer panel but never set aria‑expanded on the question button, breaking the disclosure pattern for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); in close(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ answer panel is closed, the code sets the hidden attribute but does not set aria‑hidden="true". The panel stays in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In close(item) add setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it (or set 'false') when opening.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the FAQ question buttons lack an aria‑expanded attribute, violating the requirement that toggle widgets be initialized with the correct ARIA state.
issuesFound.fix: During faqAccordion.init() or when parsing each item, set setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') for collapsed items.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator.bind() function validates fields but only calls srUtils.markInvalid() which adds aria‑describedby but never sets aria‑invalid="true". Assistive technologies cannot detect validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult() when result.valid is false, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true').

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, inlineValidator removes the error message but does not remove or set aria‑invalid="false". The field may remain marked as invalid to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult() when result.valid is true, call removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid') or setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers lack initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons that open dropdowns are given aria‑haspopup but never receive an initial aria‑expanded="false", violating the requirement that disclosure widgets be initialized.
issuesFound.fix: In register() after ensuring aria‑haspopup, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state missing aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the code adds the hidden attribute but does not set aria‑hidden="true". The drawer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it (or set 'false') when opening.

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy (NewsletterForm)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> in NewsletterForm uses the custom loading prop instead of the component’s isLoading prop, so aria‑busy is never set. Screen‑reader users cannot tell that the form is submitting, causing a blocking accessibility failure.

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy (ContactForm)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> in ContactForm also passes a non‑standard loading prop, so aria‑busy is not applied. Users of assistive technology receive no indication that the message is being sent.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1150: ```tsx
  <Button
    type="submit"
    variant="primary"
    size="md"
    isLoading={isSubmitting}
    aria-disabled={isSubmitting}
  >
    {isSubmitting ? 'Sending…' : 'Send message'}
  </Button>
  ```
